pi_items.verified_value: SpamAssassin public corpus - real, publicly available dataset from http://spamassassin.apache.org/publiccorpus/
pi_items.source: Verified via web search and email headers containing spamassassin.taint.org references and mailing list discussions (SAdev, ILUG, IIU, zzzzteana, etc.) consistent with public corpus mailing list archives

privileged_info_summary: The task uses real emails from the SpamAssassin public corpus (ham_001-050.eml), with 10 emails pre-sorted into 2 folders (Security-Discussion and Hardware-Issues, 5 emails each) and 40 remaining unsorted in the inbox. The task requires agents to create a minimum of 3 additional folders, move emails to achieve inbox zero (≤5 remaining emails), and send a status email to project-director@devteam.com. All metadata constraints are verified: status recipient is project-director@devteam.com, pre-existing folders are correct, minimum 3 new folders required, maximum 5 emails allowed to remain in inbox, and total expected folder count is 5. The emails are from genuine public mailing lists (SpamAssassin development, Linux user groups, general discussion lists) but the specific scenario and organization is synthetic.
